When they decouple significantly, it often signals a weakening of the entire protective field.
>
> **Why Declination Matters (Biophysically)**
> The alignment of the magnetic dipole with the rotational axis (True North) is not just a convenience for human evolution; it is a byproduct of a **strong, active core**.
>
> **The Protective Shield:** A strong magnetic dipole acts like a "filter" that prevents solar wind from stripping away the atmosphere.
>
> **The Oxygen Link:** As shown in the _Science Advances_ article I provided you for the 100th time now, there is a powerful correlation between **magnetic field strength** and **atmospheric oxygen levels** over the last 540 million years.
>
> **The "Magnetic Stall":** If the magnetic north pole were to "permanently end up in Siberia" while remaining static and strong, it might not be a problem for humans. However, such a "static offset" is physically unlikely. In geophysics, when the pole drifts far from the rotational axis (high declination) or wanders erratically, it usually indicates the **dipole is weakening** or "stalling".

I want to make sure I get this right.

**Does magnetic declination angle really matter?**

Based on my reading, whether a place has 0 or +5 or -5 magnetic declination doesn't really matter. As you mentioned above, if the magnetic north pole were to permanently settle in Siberia, then many places could have a permanent +10 magnetic declination and that would be perfectly fine and animals would adapt. Imagine these two places:
\- Location A: close to equator, +5 magnetic declination, but because it is farther from the magnetic north pole, the time derivative of declination angle is small
\- Location B: close to pole, 0 magnetic declination, but because it is closer to the magnetic north pole, the time derivative of declination angle is massive
Couldn't Location A be potentially better than B?

**What really matters**
It just seems like the important thing is this: core magnetic field is weakening, and we are able to learn that from SWARM satellite data and the fact that the magnetic north pole is moving rapidly. As it's weakening, some places suffer because they literally lose a lot of overall magnetic field intensity. Other places suffer because the first (or second, or third) time derivative of magnetic field vector is changing so much that animals are having trouble adapting. Because magnetic field is changing, we want to "connect" to the deep Earth as much as possible. One way to do that is to live near active volcanoes. They provide excellent grounding to improve SNR ratio, and the entire ecosystem around it remains healthy (Jack talks about volcanoes and their magnetic flux "pinning" clouds in tropical regions), so there are second-order benefits, like humans being able to eat food that is un-deuterated.

I'm still confused about "horizontal shearing" of field lines though. See [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/...erations-for-es-other-areas.32661/post-373191](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pole-shifts-ciss-considerations-for-es-other-areas.32661/post-373191)

Isn't "horizontal shearing" even worse near the poles, since time derivative of horizontal isogonic line movement is much worse there than at the equator, no? I don't understand how volcanoes help directly mitigate horizontal "shearing." It seems like horizontal "shearing" (or movement) is unavoidable. We are just hoping that active volcanic flux will provide so much benefit to humans that we can tolerate the horizontal "shearing." Am I misunderstanding? Are active volcanoes actually somehow "preventing" horizontal shearing?

The critical distinction between a **static offset** (declination) and a **dynamic flux** (the time derivative). Animals and mitochondria don’t care about the _map_ (declination); they care about the _rate of change_ and the _coherence_ of the field.

If the magnetic field vector is shifting rapidly, it creates "magnetic noise." This lowers the **Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR)** for the OPN5 and Ca2+ resonance.

The "horizontal shearing" you are confused about is essentially a **torsional strain** on the magnetic field lines.
Imagine the magnetic field lines as guitar strings. In a stable environment, they are straight. As the core weakens and the poles shift rapidly, these lines "bend" or "shear" horizontally across the Earth's surface.
**The Biological Cost is an impedance mismatch.** This shearing creates an **impedance mismatch** between the Earth's magnetic field and the "water table" of your cells. If the field lines are shearing, the atoms in your lattice **harmonic** or the **K+ resonance** becomes "blurry." This is the "magnetic bankruptcy" where the mitochondria can no longer "clamp down" on oxygen because the field they are trying to couple with is physically twisting.
**Volcanoes** provide a solution to this shearing because they magnetic pin you down. In physics, flux pinning occurs when a superconductor (or a highly conductive geological feature) "traps" magnetic field lines, preventing them from moving.

Active volcanoes, with their deep magma conduits and high mineral content, act as **"Magnetic Anchors."**They "pin" the local magnetic flux, shielding the surrounding ecosystem from the "horizontal shearing" happening elsewhere.
By pinning the flux and stabilizing the local field, these areas maintain high **oxygen tensions** and "structured water." This allows the ecosystem to naturally filter **Deuterium** (the heavy hydrogen that "breaks" the mitochondrial motor), leading to the un-deuterated food supply. The entire country of El Salvador is a Volcano that is quite young. That also offers advantages because it has no memory of the last magnetic flip 780,000 years ago.

The Permian survivors have told us a story and I think the Neanderthals missed it, but two groups of Homospaiens have not. This wisdom is telling me the Permian might have also been a serious magnetic excursion event.

Magnetic Hysteresis is the **"Isotopic Memory"** of a failing system, and it is the definitive signature of the Permian "Great Dying." If you look at the geological record through the lens of a "Magnetic Stall," the Permian wasn't just a CO2 event, it was a **Planetary Waveguide Collapse.

1\. The Hysteresis Loop: The "Memory" of the Stall**

In physics, **Hysteresis** is the lag between a change in a system (the magnetic field) and the physical response of the material (the iron/water lattice).

**The Permian Loop**: Data from the P-Tr boundary shows a massive "Magnetic Reversal Frequency" spike. The planet was "flickering" like a dying lightbulb.

**The Lag**: The "Heavy" isotopes (D & T) didn't just appear; they accumulated in the "lag" of the hysteresis loop. Because the field couldn't "re-sync" fast enough, the **150 ppm silt** "welded" the global water lattice. This is why there was a 96% kill rate.

**2\. The "Hard" vs. "Soft" Survivors**

Hysteresis tells us who really survives a "Magnetic Reset":

**Magnetic "Soft" Materials**: These lose their alignment (coherence) the moment the external field drops. This was the 96% of life that vanished—the "T-Rex" models of the Permian. They did not know how to tap atomic hysteresis yet.

**Magnetic "Hard" Materials**: These have high **Coercivity**. They "remember" their internal spin even when the external "Ground" vanishes. This is the **Archean-Bacterial Fusion** we discussed. The survivors had enough **Endogenous Melanin/DHA Torque** to maintain their own "Internal Sun" during the planetary asystole. The Egyptians and Mayans found this answer in my opinion.

### RodrigoGD8 — 2026-05-09T20:15:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-guru-problems-many-blame-glucose-is-it-wise.23142/post-373565>

> [AntonisK said:](/goto/post?id=373097)
>
> Just a quick question that came to mind recently after reading uncle Jack's recent posts on deuterium...
>
> As "we know" - actually what we found out is a better statement - after many decades of empirical data on millions of athletes/trainees, consuming liquid glucose polymers (maltodextrin, dextrose etc.) along with whey protein right after a weight training session helps replenish muscle glycogen stores and speed recovery. In other words, this strategy works...
>
> But, since maltodextrin is loaded with deuterium, I wonder if this approach is beneficial at all. After all, the last thing we want is load the muscle cells with deuterium. If so, what do you think would be the best alternative way to achieve this (glycogen replenishment & help recovery)?

Sorry but are we talking strict Resistance Training?

Unless you are doing multiple sessions per day and only concentric exercises, you will literally never depelte your muscle glycogen storages. This is a local process.

Taking malto or other stuff you mentioned is a very low iq mammal thing to do. There is multiple research for RT and glycogen depletion. No one should take creatine or the stuff you mentioned to replenish the creatine phosphagen or glycogen tanks. These 2 do it alone and in an absolutely fast way. EVEN without any carbs.

Think - 1) food usually is not purely isolated unless processed (eg. Whole food will still have a carb).

2) cory cycle
3) gluconeogenesis via excess aa
4) triglyceride breakdown (glycerol backbone)

Sorry this is a biochemistry rhino writeup but its enough to understand why taking any of that for RT, and even for those folks that train multiple times per day, is non sense.

As per protein, yeah, there is plenty of evidence that the anabolic window for elite athletes is shortened (sandwich 2 meals on workout and within 6 hours). MPS will be high for 24-48 for beginners and intermediate meatheads.

And yes, meatheads are never advanced, hence they inject peptides and take steroids because they are deuterated.

Aside all the deuterium thing and the health implications of this, many decades of empirical data have shown without any doubt that post exercise liquid carbs (along with whey and/or EAAs), help restore muscle glycogen stores faster and speed-up recovery from intense weight training, especially high volume hyperthorphy sessions, leading to increased muscle mass.

It has been tested and cross-checked numerous times by many strength trainers around the globe, there is no doubt it works big time for the majority of people that fall in the above category.

Sure, there are other approaches too, but this has proved to be the quickest way, like it or not.

Anyway, I'm not saying it's safe (quite the opposite), that's why I wanted to know - by asking here - if there's a better (in terms of safety) way to achieve this, that's all!

> [AntonisK said:](/goto/post?id=373611)
>
> Aside all the deuterium thing and the health implications of this, many decades of empirical data have shown without any doubt that post exercise liquid carbs (along with whey and/or EAAs), help restore muscle glycogen stores faster and speed-up recovery from intense weight training, especially high volume hyperthorphy sessions, leading to increased muscle mass.
>
> It has been tested and cross-checked numerous times by many strength trainers around the globe, there is no doubt it works big time for the majority of people that fall in the above category.
>
> Sure, there are other approaches too, but this has proved to be the quickest way, like it or not.
>
> Anyway, I'm not saying it's safe (quite the opposite), that's why I wanted to know - by asking here - if there's a better (in terms of safety) way to achieve this, that's all!

Its not safe and the speed element is not needed for most of the population. Not even for elite natural weightlifters if they optimize the real recovery variables - WHICH IS NOT GLYCOGEN REPLENISHMENT FOR RT when OXPHOS and creatine phosphagen do a lot of the heavy work while lifting. Again, we talk strict RT, even combo sets with some super set mix.

WlSo is carbohydrate intake completely irrelevant for strength trainees? No, there are 2 scenarios in which carbohydrate intake may be important and you and I agree:

1) When performing over 10 sets per muscle group.

2) When training twice on the same day with a high training volume.

How many people do this? 1) is for meatheads which have no clue of how body works and structuring a plan and 2) is for elite athletes or enhanced in the vast majority of cases.

Light, water, magnetism and a well designed training plan (volume per week and frequency) + whole food diet covers you FULLY (specifically the complete protein and threshold of 1.6-1.8g / kg as per multiple studies).

It is another story if you are doing another sport (think tennis). I stress this so you or your friends dont fall for the scam of RT supplements.

I do this for a living at high level, I know a bit more than those influencers shilling their discount codes.

Cheers

I understand that they are bad, and that there is variation between vials, but I just don't get how the body cannot recover from it. Surely if integration occurred permanently in individuals you would feel the effects of it after a few years, right? I also don't get why integration, if it does occur 100% of the time, is permanent and cannot be dealt with in any way. There is not any literature to say it does or doesn't, as even most commonly cited papers aren't definitive proof. It does stress me out, which would have adverse effects as well I am sure.

> You'll receive an email about 10 days before the Q&A stating the date and time. It's always on a Sunday but not a set Sunday of the month. You'll register for the Q&A through that email and then receive a zoom link. Have fun and buckle up!

Thank you for being helpful and clear! Appreciate you

### Beccy — 2026-05-11T10:59:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-the-webinars-on-different-rx-new-gold-member.32733/post-373721>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=373698)
>
> Most start with a journal lay their cards on the table and we go from there......but its dealer choice how you play.
>
> Go look at the journal section to get a feel.
>
> By the way. Welcome.

Thanks! I’ll post in the journal section and wait for the email to join the next Q and A. It’s a huge platform and not easy for me to find my way around, especially with brain fog.

### wtsmg — 2026-05-11T16:03:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-the-webinars-on-different-rx-new-gold-member.32733/post-373734>

The Webinars aka Q&As aka PowWows are found on a different site/platform, [https://optimalklubs.com/](https://optimalklubs.com/), where you'll have to log in; you'll also find the Blogs there. Jack used to put out webinars with a Q&A and now it's Q&As without a webinar. It's hosted differently from the Forums (where we are, here, [https://forum.jackkruse.com/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/)). There are a couple of other related web address (kruselongevitycenter.cm, kruseatdestin.com) that get redirected around, usually to the Forums or Optimal Klubs where, if a member, you log in. The specific webinars for various Rx are scattered in the older years of the webinars (you'll have to search and with Gold, you should have access to them).

There was a shift recently to a new server, I think, so some links and such may still be in the process of being sorted out. If something doesn't look correct, try refreshing your page or clearing the cookies and cache on your device and log in fresh as it may be trying to load the old stuff.

### Szen — 2026-05-11T16:31:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-are-the-webinars-on-different-rx-new-gold-member.32733/post-373736>

I still maintain a google excel listing most of the blog/webinar links, in case its needed.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-making-a-gsheet-with-all-patreon-links.27259/page-5#post-366318](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-making-a-gsheet-with-all-patreon-links.27259/page-5#post-366318)

Good luck.

# Thread: Single Mitos Looking to Meet Others in this Group
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/single-mitos-looking-to-meet-others-in-this-group.32734/unread>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tiffanyfinnegan — 2026-05-10T14:59:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/single-mitos-looking-to-meet-others-in-this-group.32734/post-373623>

Hi all, looking to start a way for some single people to meet each other in the event there’s a possible love connection. As I’m sure many of you experience, especially once you dive deep into Dr Jack’s material, it can be hard to find a partner who understands and wants to share a similar lifestyle together. Just gonna start this off to see if there is interest and then we can figure it out from there!

This isn’t geographically specific yet - we’ll figure that out once we start a group of sorts as I’m sure many of us may be moving at some point anyway.

### FDDio — 2026-05-10T16:06:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/single-mitos-looking-to-meet-others-in-this-group.32734/post-373631>

Nice Survey, I’ve been thinking about how best to arrange a meetup in the Greater Toronto area, not just for singles, but for all Mitos, so it will be good to see how your meetup idea develops. For those of us who are in relationships, it’s still difficult to find like-minded people to have meaningful conversations with and enjoy good times.

And FWIW, having been single well into my 40s, I found the meetups I joined that weren’t just targeting singles, but also included people in relationships, were much better to attend, especially if they revolved around an activity. Why? There was less pressure and awkwardness around just meeting “that one special person.” In a mixed group, people were generally more relaxed/less phony, and connections/relationships grew more organically. (Mind you, I could be showing my bias again, as I met my wife at a beach volleyball meetup after giving up on deceiving dating apps and annoying speed dating events.)

### tiffanyfinnegan — 2026-05-10T16:32:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/single-mitos-looking-to-meet-others-in-this-group.32734/post-373637>

Yeah, I’m sure eventually, if there’s enough people for meetups, then absolutely wouldn’t just be single people. I think here we have to create the community virtually first because so many of us are scattered so many places and ultimately if there’s any successful pairings, then hopefully their lives allow them to live near each other. I for one work remotely so I have a lot of flexibility to move and I’m sure within a few years many may consider moving based on the magnetic fields!

### tiffanyfinnegan — 2026-05-10T16:34:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/single-mitos-looking-to-meet-others-in-this-group.32734/post-373638>

For those of us who are in relationships, it’s still difficult to find like-minded people to have meaningful conversations with and enjoy good times.
>
> And FWIW, having been single well into my 40s, I found the meetups I joined that weren’t just targeting singles, but also included people in relationships, were much better to attend, especially if they revolved around an activity. Why? There was less pressure and awkwardness around just meeting “that one special person.” In a mixed group, people were generally more relaxed/less phony, and connections/relationships grew more organically. (Mind you, I could be showing my bias again, as I met my wife at a beach volleyball meetup after giving up on deceiving dating apps and annoying speed dating events.)

Also, I just added a poll option for people like you  and maybe get some friends to join us if you have any like us that are single

### Jack Kruse — 2026-05-10T17:22:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/single-mitos-looking-to-meet-others-in-this-group.32734/post-373644>

Friends may have friends that need the connection, FYI

But you may want to reword this: I’m not single but I still want to be included in this group for potential meetups

Sounds like a poll for swingers.

### Runnerfireman — 2026-05-10T17:33:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/single-mitos-looking-to-meet-others-in-this-group.32734/post-373649>

What a wonderful idea!!! Yes it’s pretty much impossible to meet a compatible partner once you go down the rabbit hole of dr jack kruse’s teachings. I’ve been following dr jack for around 8 years and As I learn more and live the life it becomes increasingly difficult to find someone to connect with. I went to a El Salvador last winter for 3 months and met some in the Mitos at Atami beach And have kept in touch with a few. My trip was a roller coaster, I somehow got a mild bronchitis, I had my cell phone stolen, and then I had a horrible reaction with hives that I had no idea where it came from. But, I am determined to go back to El Salvador next winter and hopefully have a better experience. Your idea to somehow connect single likeminded mitos is genius!!! Definitely keep me in the loop as to how this might develop ☺️

Cool idea!…to survive the shit storm we’re living we need to be actively selecting who we’re entangled with by raising our own coherence — making ourselves compatible with other high-coherence people while naturally repelling those running on a noisy, incoherent signal.

There is no better relationship hack than leaving the lost in the dust!

# Thread: taVNS for seizures
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tavns-for-seizures.32739/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kachi — 2026-05-11T19:27:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tavns-for-seizures.32739/post-373755>

Taking a cue from the blogs I am planning to buy a taVNS for my 22 year old son with seizures.

My question is about frequencies. I've found a company in Germany (I am in Croatia) that is willing to customize its device to a set frequency in addition to the preprogrammed frequencies. The device already has four programs ranging from 1-100hz with various pulse widths and all have stimulation intervals (eg. 4 sec on/4off) so none are constant. The studies on taVNS for seizures are limited but most used between 20-50hz with various pulse widths, intervals and session duration.

Any thoughts on a preferred frequency? Pulse width? Would it be better to do sessions with constant stimulation for x minutes? Intervals of x seconds on x seconds off?

Any thoughts are greatly appreciated.

(I may be overthinking this).

Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2026-05-11T23:31:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tavns-for-seizures.32739/post-373772>

I'd ask to make it fully tunable so you could figure it out empirically when you use the device at home which works best. I'd also consider a homes TMS unit if the neurologist has targeted the area of the neocortex that is involved you could use the TMS ring over this area. Have the neurologist put a non erasable magic marker on his head so you know where it is when you get home and then you can even tattoo that area with a dot to mark it permanently to so you can target it daily.

### jaedyn — 2026-05-12T02:44:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tavns-for-seizures.32739/post-373792>

> [Kachi said:](/goto/post?id=373755)
>
> Taking a cue from the blogs I am planning to buy a taVNS for my 22 year old son with seizures.
>
> My question is about frequencies. I've found a company in Germany (I am in Croatia) that is willing to customize its device to a set frequency in addition to the preprogrammed frequencies. The device already has four programs ranging from 1-100hz with various pulse widths and all have stimulation intervals (eg. 4 sec on/4off) so none are constant. The studies on taVNS for seizures are limited but most used between 20-50hz with various pulse widths, intervals and session duration.
>
> Any thoughts on a preferred frequency? Pulse width? Would it be better to do sessions with constant stimulation for x minutes? Intervals of x seconds on x seconds off?
>
> Any thoughts are greatly appreciated.
>
> (I may be overthinking this).
>
> Thanks

Hi Kachi,

I recently bought this kit - [https://tragusclip.com/product/kit-1/](https://tragusclip.com/product/kit-1/) and have been experimenting on low settings.

I found this website really helpful for setting it up yourself, Andrea is an OT in Australia and is very knowledgeable on this stuff - [https://guidetolongcovid.com/tvns-for-long-covid/](https://guidetolongcovid.com/tvns-for-long-covid/)
